# Landing Redesign Build — Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED SUB-SKILL: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (recommended) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan task-by-task. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Rebuild `danilorojas.design` as a single-column, light-editorial portfolio that leads with thinking — per `laboratorio/superpowers/specs/2026-06-14-landing-redesign.md` — driven by `perfil/data/` and the Paso-1 repositioning, with all stale copy purged.

**Architecture:** The landing is generated by `generadores/v11-landing.ts` → `generadores/templates/v11-landing/*` reading `perfil/data/*.yaml`. Today most landing copy is **hardcoded** in `index.ts` (the `COPY` object + `renderProof` items), not data-driven. This plan (a) adds two small data shapes (`notes`, `trustStrip`) with validators + TDD, (b) makes hero/proof/trust data-driven, (c) re-themes `design-system/tokens-web.css` dark→light editorial and self-hosts fonts, (d) restructures the template tabs→single-column scroll with the new section order and purges stale strings, (e) verifies build + tests + deploy routes.

**Tech Stack:** TypeScript (ESM, `tsx`), Vitest, js-yaml, hand-written HTML/CSS template strings. No framework. Output to `dist/landing-v11/`.

**Conventions:** Edit source only — never `dist/` by hand. Deploy routes that MUST keep working: `dist/landing-v11/{index.html, es/, work/*, CNAME, app/, daily/, plan/}`. Backup of the pre-redesign landing: `laboratorio/landing-v11-backup-2026-06-14/`.

**Out of scope (per spec §7):** MentorOS/`/app`; the dark app cockpit; net-new case-study *content* (Juan Valdez / Credit Score / FAA cases are a separate Q&A effort); any reference to the private $250k goal.

## Phase 0 — Prerequisites

### Task 0.1: Branch + install deps

**Files:** none (environment)

```bash
git checkout -b landing-redesign
```

```bash
npm install
```
Expected: completes; `node_modules/.bin/tsx` and `vitest` exist.

Run:
```bash
npm test && npm run build:landing-v11
```
Expected: tests PASS; build writes `dist/landing-v11/index.html`. If the build fails on the current data (it may, after Paso-1 edits), note the error — Phase 3 fixes the renderer. If only the build fails (not tests), proceed; if tests fail, stop and report.

- [ ] **Step 4: Commit the baseline marker**

```bash
git add -A && git commit -m "chore: branch for landing redesign"
```

## Phase 1 — Data layer: `notes` + `trustStrip` + hero line (TDD)

New content is data-driven. Shapes:

- `Note`: `{ slug, titleEn, titleEs, bodyEn, bodyEs }` — the POV notes.
- `trustStrip`: bilingual ordered string lists on `positioning`.
- `heroLine`: bilingual short hero copy on `positioning` (the thesis stays for CV/about).

### Task 1.1: Extend types

**Files:**
- Modify: `generadores/lib/types.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add the `Note` interface and extend `Positioning` + `LandingData`**

In `generadores/lib/types.ts`, add after the `Positioning` interface:

```typescript
// ============= NOTES (POV) =============
export interface Note {
  slug: string;
  titleEn: string;
  titleEs: string;
  bodyEn: string;
  bodyEs: string;
}
export type Notes = Note[];
```

Extend the `Positioning` interface to add two optional fields:

```typescript
export interface Positioning {
  thesis: { en: string; es: string };
  tagline: { en: string; es: string };
  heroLine?: { en: string; es: string };
  trustStrip?: { en: string[]; es: string[] };
  thesisBairesdev?: { en: string };
  proofNumbers: Array<{
    value: string;
    unit?: string;
    labelEn: string;
    labelEs: string;
  }>;
}
```

Extend `LandingData`:

```typescript
export interface LandingData extends CvData {
  landing: Landing;
  horizon: HorizonSection;
  notes: Notes;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Typecheck**

Run: `npx tsc --noEmit`
Expected: errors only in `load-data.ts`/`index.ts` (they don't yet provide `notes`) — resolved in 1.2 and 3.x. No errors inside `types.ts`.

### Task 1.2: Validators + loaders (TDD)

**Files:**
- Modify: `generadores/lib/load-data.ts`
- Test: `tests/load-data.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Write failing tests**

Append to `tests/load-data.test.ts`:

```typescript
import { loadNotes, validateNotes } from "../generadores/lib/load-data.js";

describe("validateNotes", () => {
  it("parses a list of notes", () => {
    const notes = validateNotes([
      { slug: "a", titleEn: "T", titleEs: "T", bodyEn: "B", bodyEs: "B" },
    ]);
    expect(notes).toHaveLength(1);
    expect(notes[0].slug).toBe("a");
  });
  it("rejects a note missing a field", () => {
    expect(() => validateNotes([{ slug: "a", titleEn: "T" }])).toThrow();
  });
});

describe("validatePositioning trustStrip + heroLine", () => {
  it("accepts trustStrip and heroLine when present", () => {
    const p = validatePositioning({
      thesis: { en: "x", es: "x" },
      tagline: { en: "x", es: "x" },
      heroLine: { en: "h", es: "h" },
      trustStrip: { en: ["A", "B"], es: ["A", "B"] },
      proofNumbers: [{ value: "1", labelEn: "L", labelEs: "L" }],
    });
    expect(p.heroLine?.en).toBe("h");
    expect(p.trustStrip?.es).toEqual(["A", "B"]);
  });
  it("omits them when absent", () => {
    const p = validatePositioning({
      thesis: { en: "x", es: "x" },
      tagline: { en: "x", es: "x" },
      proofNumbers: [{ value: "1", labelEn: "L", labelEs: "L" }],
    });
    expect(p.heroLine).toBeUndefined();
    expect(p.trustStrip).toBeUndefined();
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run — verify failure**

Run: `npx vitest run tests/load-data.test.ts`
Expected: FAIL — `loadNotes`/`validateNotes` not exported; trustStrip/heroLine not returned.

- [ ] **Step 3: Implement in `load-data.ts`**

Add `Note`, `Notes` to the type import block at top. In `validatePositioning`, before the `return`, add:

```typescript
  let heroLine: Positioning["heroLine"] = undefined;
  if (o.heroLine !== undefined && o.heroLine !== null) {
    const hl = requireObject(o.heroLine, "positioning.heroLine");
    heroLine = {
      en: requireString(hl.en, "positioning.heroLine.en"),
      es: requireString(hl.es, "positioning.heroLine.es"),
    };
  }
  let trustStrip: Positioning["trustStrip"] = undefined;
  if (o.trustStrip !== undefined && o.trustStrip !== null) {
    const ts = requireObject(o.trustStrip, "positioning.trustStrip");
    trustStrip = {
      en: requireArray(ts.en, "positioning.trustStrip.en", (s, i) => requireString(s, `positioning.trustStrip.en[${i}]`)),
      es: requireArray(ts.es, "positioning.trustStrip.es", (s, i) => requireString(s, `positioning.trustStrip.es[${i}]`)),
    };
  }
```

and add `heroLine,` and `trustStrip,` to the returned object.

Add near the other validators:

```typescript
export function validateNotes(raw: unknown): Note[] {
  return requireArray(raw, "notes", (n, i) => {
    const o = requireObject(n, `notes[${i}]`);
    return {
      slug: requireString(o.slug, `notes[${i}].slug`),
      titleEn: requireString(o.titleEn, `notes[${i}].titleEn`),
      titleEs: requireString(o.titleEs, `notes[${i}].titleEs`),
      bodyEn: requireString(o.bodyEn, `notes[${i}].bodyEn`),
      bodyEs: requireString(o.bodyEs, `notes[${i}].bodyEs`),
    };
  });
}

export function loadNotes(filePath: string): Note[] {
  return loadYaml(filePath, validateNotes);
}
```

In `loadLandingData`, load notes (tolerate a missing file so the build never hard-fails on an empty notes set):

```typescript
export function loadLandingData(dataDir: string): LandingData {
  const cv = loadCvData(dataDir);
  const landing = loadLanding(path.join(dataDir, "landing.yaml"));
  const horizon = loadHorizon(path.join(dataDir, "horizon.yaml"));
  const notesPath = path.join(dataDir, "notes.yaml");
  const notes = existsSync(notesPath) ? loadNotes(notesPath) : [];
  return { ...cv, landing, horizon, notes };
}
```

Expected: PASS.

- [ ] **Step 1: Create `perfil/data/notes.yaml`** (the two approved v1 notes; bodies verbatim from spec §5.4)

```yaml
- slug: teams-do-not-lack-solutions
  titleEn: "Most teams do not lack solutions."
  titleEs: "A la mayoría de los equipos no les faltan soluciones."
  bodyEn: "Teams rarely fail for lack of ideas, UI options, or tools. They fail because the problem was misdiagnosed, the constraints were never made explicit, or they shipped around the real decision. Good design reduces ambiguity before it produces screens."
  bodyEs: "Los equipos rara vez fallan por falta de ideas, opciones de UI o herramientas. Fallan porque el problema se diagnosticó mal, las restricciones nunca se hicieron explícitas, o entregaron alrededor de la decisión real. El buen diseño reduce la ambigüedad antes de producir pantallas."
- slug: handoff-judgment-disappears
  titleEn: "Handoff is where judgment disappears."
  titleEs: "El handoff es donde desaparece el juicio."
  bodyEn: "Traditional handoff turns product decisions into artifacts and asks another team to reconstruct the intent later — that gap is where waste lives. The answer isn't designers pretending to be engineers; it's making judgment executable through systems, rubrics, and agents."
  bodyEs: "El handoff tradicional convierte decisiones de producto en artefactos y le pide a otro equipo reconstruir la intención después — ese hueco es donde vive el desperdicio. La respuesta no es diseñadores fingiendo ser ingenieros; es hacer el juicio ejecutable mediante sistemas, rúbricas y agentes."
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add `heroLine` + `trustStrip` to `perfil/data/positioning.yaml`** (after `tagline:`)

```yaml
heroLine:
  en: "Designing products for complex systems, regulated environments, and teams that need to move faster."
  es: "Diseñando productos para sistemas complejos, entornos regulados y equipos que necesitan moverse más rápido."
trustStrip:
  en: ["FAA", "DoD", "VA", "Booz Allen Hamilton", "Pichincha Bank", "Rappi", "Juan Valdez"]
  es: ["FAA", "DoD", "VA", "Booz Allen Hamilton", "Banco Pichincha", "Rappi", "Juan Valdez"]
```

## Phase 2 — Theme: light editorial tokens + self-hosted fonts

Visual task — verified by build + eyeball, not TDD. Hold the contrast guardrail: body text ≥ 4.5:1 on cream; accent terracotta `#D9663F` is AA on cream for large/UI use.

### Task 2.1: Re-theme `tokens-web.css` to light editorial

**Files:**
- Modify: `design-system/tokens-web.css`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace the dark SURFACE + INK token values with the light profile.** Keep the `--v11-*` names (the template references them); only change values. Target contract:

```css
/* SURFACE — light editorial */
--v11-bg-deep:       #EFE8DA;
--v11-bg-primary:    #F6F1E7;   /* cream paper — page background */
--v11-bg-alt:        #FBF8F1;   /* section alt */
--v11-bg-paper:      #FFFFFF;   /* elevated reading card */
--v11-bg-elevated:   #FFFFFF;
--v11-bg-elevated-2: #F3ECDD;
--v11-bg-inset:      #F0E9DA;

/* INK — on cream */
--v11-text:        #1C1A17;
--v11-text-body:   #2C2925;
--v11-text-85:     #3A3630;
--v11-text-70:     #57514a;
--v11-text-60:     #6B645A;
--v11-text-50:     #837b70;
--v11-text-40:     #9a9286;
--v11-text-subtle: #b6ad9f;
```

Set the accent and any line/border tokens further down the file: accent `--v11-accent: #D9663F` (verify the existing accent token name and update its value); borders/hairlines → `#DAD2C3`. Audit the rest of the file for hardcoded dark hexes (shadows, glows, code wells) and bring them into the light scale. Remove dark-only effects that read as heavy on cream.

- [ ] **Step 2: Build + eyeball**

Run: `npm run build:landing-v11 && open dist/landing-v11/index.html`
Expected: page renders on cream with dark ink and a single terracotta accent; no black panels. (Layout may still be the old structure — that's Phase 3.)

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit**

```bash
git add design-system/tokens-web.css
git commit -m "feat(ds): light editorial token profile for landing"
```

## Phase 3 — Template restructure (single-column, new order, purge stale copy)

The spine of the redesign. Section order: **Hero → Trust strip → Proof → Notes → Work → Method → About → Contact.** Verified by build + eyeball + the Phase 4 string-guard test. Each task is one section; keep each `render*` function focused.

### Task 3.1: Purge stale copy + data-drive SEO/hero/proof

**Files:**
- Modify: `generadores/templates/v11-landing/index.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Hero from data.** Rewrite `renderHero` to use `positioning.heroLine` (fallback to `thesis`) for the H1, drop the `eyebrow`/`shortThesis`/`v11-artifact`/`v11-stream` blocks entirely (no artifact viewer, no fake terminal, no particles). Keep name, the hero line, availability, and one CTA (`#contact`). Remove `roleCanonical = "Agentic Designer · Product Engineer"` and `cv-serious.en.pdf`/`generated 08 May` strings.

- [ ] **Step 2: Proof from data.** Rewrite `renderProof` to map `positioning.proofNumbers` (now the four Paso-1 numbers) instead of the hardcoded `items` array. Render `value` + optional `unit` + `labelEn/labelEs`. Delete the hardcoded `15+ / 346 / 1.0 / 95.6` block and its `attr` strings (EnRegla etc.).

- [ ] **Step 3: SEO from data.** In `renderV11Landing`, replace the hardcoded `seoTitle`/`seoDesc`/`ogAlt` ("Agentic Designer", "Fifteen years…", "Staff / Principal") with `data.landing.seo.*` and `data.identity.role`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Build + eyeball**

Run: `npm run build:landing-v11`
Expected: build OK; hero shows the new line; proof shows 18+/15/4/50+.

- [ ] **Step 5: Commit** `git commit -am "feat(landing): data-driven hero, proof, SEO; purge stale hero copy"`

### Task 3.2: Trust strip section (new)

**Files:**
- Modify: `generadores/templates/v11-landing/index.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Add `renderTrustStrip(positioning, lang)`** — text only, no logos. Render `positioning.trustStrip[lang]` joined with a ` · ` separator inside a restrained `<section class="v11-trust">`. If `trustStrip` is absent, return `""`.

```typescript
function renderTrustStrip(positioning: Positioning, lang: Lang): string {
  const items = lang === "en" ? positioning.trustStrip?.en : positioning.trustStrip?.es;
  if (!items || items.length === 0) return "";
  const label = lang === "en" ? "Trusted by teams at" : "Trabajo para equipos en";
  const row = items.map((s) => `<span class="v11-trust__item">${escapeHtml(s)}</span>`).join('<span class="v11-trust__sep" aria-hidden="true"> · </span>');
  return `<section class="v11-trust" aria-label="${escapeHtml(label)}">
  <div class="v11-container"><div class="v11-trust__row">${row}</div></div>
</section>`;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add minimal styles** for `.v11-trust`, `.v11-trust__row`, `.v11-trust__item`, `.v11-trust__sep` in `v11-styles.ts` (mono, muted, small, wrapping, generous vertical padding).

- [ ] **Step 3: Wire into `renderV11Landing`** right after `renderHero(...)` and before `renderProof(...)`.

- [ ] **Step 4: Build + eyeball; Commit** `git commit -am "feat(landing): text-only trust strip after hero"`

### Task 3.3: Notes section (replace notebook essay)

**Files:**
- Modify: `generadores/templates/v11-landing/index.ts`

- [ ] **Step 1: Replace `renderNotebook` with `renderNotes(data, lang, photoHref)`** that iterates `data.notes` (each: small mono index, serif title, one tight paragraph). Delete the `COPY.notebook` object (the "Trust is the unit of economy" essay, the `tocEn`, the "fifteen years" body paragraphs). Section id stays `#notebook` OR rename to `#notes` (update nav links in `renderNav` accordingly — pick one and keep it consistent).

```typescript
function renderNotes(data: LandingData, lang: Lang): string {
  if (data.notes.length === 0) return "";
  const eyebrow = lang === "en" ? "Notes" : "Notas";
  const items = data.notes.map((n, i) => {
    const title = lang === "en" ? n.titleEn : n.titleEs;
    const body = lang === "en" ? n.bodyEn : n.bodyEs;
    const idx = String(i + 1).padStart(2, "0");
    return `<article class="v11-note" data-reveal>
    <div class="v11-note__idx">${idx}</div>
    <h3 class="v11-note__title">${escapeHtml(title)}</h3>
    <p class="v11-note__body">${escapeHtml(body)}</p>
  </article>`;
  }).join("\n");
  return `<section id="notes" class="v11-section v11-section--paper" aria-labelledby="notes-h">
  <div class="v11-container-narrow">
    <div class="v11-section__eyebrow">${escapeHtml(eyebrow)}</div>
    <h2 id="notes-h" class="v11-h1">${lang === "en" ? "The writing is evidence of judgment." : "La escritura es evidencia de juicio."}</h2>
    <div class="v11-notes">${items}</div>
  </div>
</section>`;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Add `.v11-note*` styles** in `v11-styles.ts` (serif body, mono index, big spacing). Reuse `.v11-section--paper`/`.v11-container-narrow` (already exist).

- [ ] **Step 3: Build + eyeball; Commit** `git commit -am "feat(landing): POV Notes section, remove old notebook essay"`

Remove the `COPY.method` title "Profile plus agents. Diff as the contract." → replace with calm copy. Keep an optional verified testimonial if one exists.

Fold it into Method (call it inside `renderMethod`) and remove the standalone call, so past/present/future reads as "how I improve". Update `horizon.yaml` copy if it references the private goal (it does not today, but confirm — no $250k).

## Phase 4 — Tests, verification, deploy safety

### Task 4.1: Template guard test (no stale strings; new sections present)

**Files:**
- Test: `tests/landing.test.ts` (create)

- [ ] **Step 1: Write the test**

```typescript
import { describe, it, expect } from "vitest";
import path from "node:path";
import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url";
import { loadLandingData } from "../generadores/lib/load-data.js";
import { renderV11Landing } from "../generadores/templates/v11-landing/index.js";

const __dirname = path.dirname(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url));
const dataDir = path.join(__dirname, "..", "perfil", "data");
const tokens = "";

function html(lang: "en" | "es") {
  const data = loadLandingData(dataDir);
  return renderV11Landing(data, lang, tokens, { photoHref: "x.jpg", ogImageUrl: "https://x/og.png" });
}

describe("landing redesign", () => {
  const en = html("en");
  it("renders the new hero line", () => {
    expect(en).toContain("complex systems");
  });
  it("renders the trust strip names", () => {
    expect(en).toContain("Booz Allen Hamilton");
    expect(en).toContain("Juan Valdez");
  });
  it("renders the four proof numbers from data", () => {
    expect(en).toContain("50");
    expect(en).toContain("18");
  });
  it("renders the two POV notes", () => {
    expect(en).toContain("Most teams do not lack solutions");
    expect(en).toContain("Handoff is where judgment disappears");
  });
  it("has no stale strings", () => {
    for (const bad of ["Agentic Designer", "EnRegla", "Staff / Principal", "$250", "résumé", "Fifteen years"]) {
      expect(en).not.toContain(bad);
    }
  });
  it("uses no Google Fonts CDN", () => {
    expect(en).not.toContain("fonts.googleapis");
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 2: Run — iterate until green**

Run: `npx vitest run tests/landing.test.ts`
Expected: PASS. If "no stale strings" fails, grep the offending render function and remove the literal.

- [ ] **Step 3: Commit** `git commit -am "test(landing): guard new sections + no stale copy"`

### Task 4.2: Full verification

Run: `npm test`
Expected: PASS. Fix any fixture drift (e.g. `templates.test.ts` fixtures may need `notes: []` if a shared fixture flows into landing render — update fixtures, not production types).

- [ ] **Step 2: Typecheck**

Run: `npx tsc --noEmit`
Expected: no errors.

- [ ] **Step 3: Build all + byte budget**

Run: `npm run build:landing-v11`
Expected: every `[v11-landing] wrote …` line under the 400 KB budget; EN + ES + per-case pages emitted.

- [ ] **Step 4: Mojibake + stale scan on output**

```bash
grep -rlE "Ã|â€|EnRegla|Agentic Designer|\\$250" dist/landing-v11/index.html dist/landing-v11/es/index.html || echo "clean"
```
Expected: `clean`.

- [ ] **Step 5: Deploy routes intact**

```bash
ls dist/landing-v11/index.html dist/landing-v11/es/index.html dist/landing-v11/work dist/landing-v11/CNAME dist/landing-v11/app/index.html dist/landing-v11/daily/index.html dist/landing-v11/plan/index.html
```

- [ ] **Step 6: Eyeball EN + ES + one case page** in a browser; check light theme, order, reduced-motion (no jank), keyboard focus, copy-email.

## Follow-ups (separate efforts, not this plan)

- **Case content refresh** — the three existing cases (`enregla`, `developer-portal`, `life-update-mobile`) carry the old branding/positioning. Replace with the real portfolio cases (Juan Valdez, Credit Score, Money transfer, FAA personas, Rappi-study) via the Q&A interview modeled on the best (spec §5.5). Update `CASE_BEATS` and `sortedCases` order accordingly.
- **CV regeneration** — `generadores/cv.ts` shares `perfil/data`; rebuild and review `dist/cvs/*` after the data changes (`npm run build:cvs`). Verify the same purge (no EnRegla brand, 18/15, role line).
- **Dark toggle** — optional later; the light profile is the identity.
- **Confirm "50+ product initiatives"** is a number Danilo stands behind publicly.

## Self-review notes

- **Spec coverage:** strategy/internal-external (§0 → Tasks 3.1/3.4/3.5 purge + no-$250k guard 4.1); positioning (§1 → 1.3, 3.1); visual direction light/type-led/no-particles (§2 → Phase 2, 3.1); hook A (§3 → carried in hero/method copy, eyeballed); narrative model + order (§4 → 3.6 order, each section task); section spec + data sources (§5 → Phase 1 data + Phase 3 sections); build contract (§6 → all of Phase 2–3); out-of-scope (§7 → Follow-ups). Trust strip + notes data shapes (§8 open items) → Phase 1.
- **Placeholders:** CSS/template tasks are spec-driven + build/eyeball-verified by design (visual work), with exact files, anchors, and the token contract given; data-layer tasks carry full code + TDD. This is intentional, not a gap.
- **Type consistency:** `Note`/`Notes`, `positioning.heroLine`, `positioning.trustStrip`, `LandingData.notes`, `loadNotes`/`validateNotes`, `renderTrustStrip`/`renderNotes`/`renderAbout` are used consistently across tasks.
